Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement And Mutual Release
Off Calendar May-18-2016 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-18-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Stier for (petitioner) Elizabeth McDermott. Jamay Lee & Mary Pryce (via court call) for (petitioner) Elizabeth McDermott. Frank Cialone for Erika Parrino. Christopher Lamerdin for the State of California.

The Court stated that this is a petition to approve a settlement agreement, a petition for probate with Ms. McDermott seeking appointment, confirmed that there are no objections, that the issue is that we have an unwitnessed non holographic will and that we need clear evidence that it represents what the decedent intended. Ms. Lee stated that they submitted a declaration and points and authorities, that all parties have been provided with notice and that they have consents. The Court stated that with the evidence before the court it will appoint Ms. McDermott and will approve the settlement agreement and that it does not have an order on the petition for probate. Ms. Stier provided the order. The Court granted the petition for letters of administration with will annexed and the petition for approval of settlement agreement and mutual release (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement And Mutual Release
Off Calendar May-03-2016 Continued To May-10-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Stier for (petitioner) Elizabeth McDermott. Jamay Lee (via court call) for (petitioner) Elizabeth McDermott. Patrick McCabe for (trustee/beneficiary) Erica Perrino.

The Court stated that the various questions regarding notice have been cleared up and that Ms. Touchatt was not a beneficiary under the 2012 will but is provided for under the settlement agreement which she did not sign. Mr. McCabe stated that Ms. Touchatt was involved in the drafts of the estate plan. The Court stated that she and everyone else have had notice and opportunity to object, that a substantive question is that the petition to appoint Ms. McDermott is not being heard until 5/10/16 and that this agreement is not effective until the appointment. Ms. Lee stated that they tried to have the petitions heard on the same day but were told that it was not possible. The Court stated that the concern is that someone could object to the appointment between now and 5/10/16. Ms. Lee stated that all of the people entitled to notice for the appointment are the same persons for this petition. The Court stated that it just does not know what might happen by then and asked what the downside would be to continuing this petition to 5/10/16. Ms. Lee stated that it would be a great idea. The petition for approval of settlement agreement and mutual release was continued to May 10, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to coincide with the hearing on the appointment.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
